suppress.notes:
CVE-2025-7962 is an SMTP-injection vulnerability in the SMTP transport code of angus-mail (the "smtp" and "jakarta.mail" artifacts). It does not apply to angus-activation, a separate artifact in the same Eclipse Angus project that implements the Jakarta Activation Framework and contains no SMTP/mail-transport code at all.
Dependency-check matches this CVE to angus-activation via a shared CPE (cpe:2.3:a:eclipse:angus_mail), which is a known false-positive pattern reported upstream: https://github.com/dependency-check/DependencyCheck/issues/8144 https://github.com/dependency-check/DependencyCheck/issues/8211
Confirmed via Maven Central's own directory listing that no 2.0.4 (or later non-milestone) release of angus-activation exists; the 2.0.4 fix landed only in the smtp/jakarta.mail artifacts, so there is nothing to upgrade to here even if this were a real finding for this artifact. This app declares no mail dependency (no spring-boot-starter-mail, no javax.mail/jakarta.mail usage, no JavaMailSender anywhere in the codebase, confirmed by search), so angus-activation is present only as someone else's transitive dependency and its SMTP-adjacent module is not reachable from this application regardless.
